LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GEORGE VALDES, STEPHEN BURKE AND UNITED SCREENERS ASSOCIATION LOCAL ONE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT CANNOT CONCLUDE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT THE STATEMENTS WERE UNDERSTOOD AS HYBERPOLIC JOKES. DENIED AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY REJECTED THIS ARGUMENT; THE FLYER CAN BE VIEWED AS A SOLICITATION OF SERVICES AND CC SECTION 3344 (D) IS NOT APPLICABLE. GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION BY PLAINTIFFS AGAINST USA, LOCAL 1 AND BURKE; AND 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS. THESE CLAIMS ARE DUPLICATIVE OF THE DEFAMATION CLAIM. DINAPOLI IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND. GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. DINAPOLI DOES NOT ALLEGE A RELATIONSHIP WITH PLAINTIFF. GRANT WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AS DUPLICATIVE OF DEFAMATION CLAIM. DINAPOLI HAS LEAVE TO AMEND BASED ON HARASSING CONDUCT. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Aug-13-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Anne Yen of Weinberg Roger & Rosenfeld (510.337.1001) for plaintiff and responding party
Stephen Sommers (839.8569) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS GEORGE VALDES, STEPHEN BURKE AND UNITED SCREENERS ASSOCIATION LOCAL ONE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is denied as to the 1st cause of action. The Court cannot conclude as a matter of law that the statements were understood as hyberpolic jokes. Denied as to the 5th cause of action. The Court previously rejected this argument; the flyer can be viewed as a solicitation of services and CC section 3344 (d) is not applicable. Grant without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action by plaintiffs against USA, Local 1 and Burke; and 3rd and 4th causes of action against defendants. These claims are duplicative of the defamation claim. Dinapoli is granted leave to amend. Grant without leave to amend as 6th cause of action. Dinapoli does not allege a relationship with plaintiff. Grant without leave to amend as to intentional infliction of emotional distress as duplicative of defamation claim. Dinapoli has leave to amend based on harassing conduct. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
